managedBadge.managedTooltip: Credential lifecycle and registered tool calls are managed by Parsar and included in audit.
managedBadge.unmanagedTooltip: Parsar does not inject or audit this behavior directly. Review sandbox logs and external systems if needed.

page.title: Models
page.description: Org-shared model catalog. Anyone can add a model; only the creator (or an admin) can edit or disable it.

actions.editForbidden: Only the creator or an admin can edit this model.
actions.disableForbidden: Only the creator or an admin can disable this model.

copy.secretInaccessible: The original Secret isn't visible to you. Pick another Secret or paste a fresh key.

loadError.title: Failed to load Models
loadError.hint: Check the server logs or retry.

unreachable.title: Cannot reach Parsar server
unreachable.description: Frontend can't talk to the backend API (/api/v1/...). The server may be offline, the port busy, or the Vite proxy misconfigured.
unreachable.hint: Check that the Go server is running on :8080 (`make server` or `make dev`). Retry only re-sends the request, it does not fix the server.

empty.title: No shared models yet
empty.description: Register a model so agents (and your teammates) can use it. Pick a credential mode based on whether you want to share one key with the whole org, or have each user provide their own.
empty.descriptionShort: No models match the current filter.

disable.title: Disable model "{{name}}"?
disable.description: Disabling marks the model unusable for new requests. In-flight runs continue. Re-enable from the catalog later.

test.unsupported: Connectivity test only supports OpenAI / Anthropic-compatible providers

fields.name: Display name
fields.modelKeyCatalogHint: Pick from this provider's known models, or type any model id.
fields.modelKeySearch: Search or type a model id…
fields.modelKeyEmpty: No matching models — type any id

inlineSecret.title: Shared key (org-wide)
inlineSecret.apiKeyHint: We'll encrypt the key into a Secret behind the scenes. You can reuse an existing Secret instead.

credentialRef.title: Personal key (per user)
credentialRef.kindHint: Users will fill in their key from "My Credentials" using this kind code.

credentialBinding.rotateHint: Leave blank to keep the current Secret. Pasting a value creates a fresh Secret behind the scenes.
credentialBinding.rotateExplain: We'll encrypt the new key into a Secret and re-point the model at it. The old Secret stays around so audit / rollback is still possible.
credentialBinding.kindCodeHint: Each user supplies their own credential from "My Credentials" under this kind.

page.title: Inbox / Approvals
page.description: Where Agent actions that need human judgment land. Keeps risky moves out of IM threads and into a place you can audit.

detail.alreadyDecided: This approval has been handled — actions are no longer available.
detail.empty: Select an approval to see details

empty.title: No approvals
empty.description: When an Agent tries a high-risk action, it lands here for your call.

loadError.title: Failed to load approvals
loadError.hint: Make sure the server is running and the current workspace exists.

unreachable.title: Cannot reach the Parsar server
unreachable.description: The frontend cannot access the backend API (/api/v1/...). The server might be down, the port might be occupied, or the Vite proxy is misconfigured.
unreachable.hint: Check that the backend is running on port 18080: `make server` or `make dev`.

mockBanner.hint: Choose the matching Workspace in the header.

page.title: Conversations
page.description: Chat with your team's Agents here.

sidebar.emptyForAgent: No conversations yet. Click + New above to start one.
sidebar.renameTooLong: Title must be at most 200 characters
sidebar.deleteConfirmDesc: Are you sure you want to delete "{{title}}"? The conversation and its chat history will no longer appear in the list (audit records are preserved).

empty.greet: What would you like to talk about?
empty.placeholderNoAgent: Pick an Agent to start chatting

fields.title: Title (optional)
fields.titlePlaceholder: Leave blank to auto-name from the first 30 chars of your first message

runtime_error.capability_credential_missing: Capability {{name}} failed to start: missing {{kind}} credential.
runtime_error.capability_credential_decrypt_failed: Capability {{name}} credential could not be decrypted. Please contact an administrator.
runtime_error.capability_credential_kind_mismatch: Capability {{name}} credential type does not match. Please set it again.
runtime_error.capability_version_unavailable: Capability {{name}} has no usable upload yet. Re-upload it as a zip, pick a newer version, or switch the binding to 'latest'.
runtime_error.retryHint: After setting the credential, send your request again. Parsar will not retry automatically.
runtime_error.generic: A runtime capability failed to start. Please retry later or contact an administrator.

sandboxGuard.checking: Checking this Agent's sandbox before sending…
sandboxGuard.missing: This Agent does not have a healthy sandbox yet. Provision or rebuild its sandbox from the Agent detail page before sending a message.
sandboxGuard.notLive: This Agent's sandbox is not ready ({{status}}). Rebuild it from the Agent detail page before sending a message.
sandboxGuard.error: Could not check this Agent's sandbox health: {{error}}

loadError.title: Failed to load conversation
loadError.hint: Make sure the server is running and the current workspace exists.

unreachable.title: Cannot reach the Parsar server

page.title: Runs
page.description: Each Agent execution is a Run. Pinpoint what's running, stuck, failed, or waiting for approval.

cancel.label: Cancel
cancel.confirmBody: Cancel this run? This cannot be undone.

loadError.title: Failed to load runs
loadError.hint: Make sure the server is running and the current workspace exists in the dev seed.

unreachable.title: Cannot reach the Parsar server
unreachable.description: The frontend cannot access the backend API (/api/v1/...). The server might be down, the port might be occupied, or the Vite proxy is misconfigured.
unreachable.hint: Check that the backend is running on port 18080: `make server` or `make dev`. Retrying just sends the request again; it does not fix the server.

mockBanner.title: Choose a data scope
mockBanner.hint: Choose the matching Workspace in the header.

empty.title: No agent runs yet
empty.description: @Agent in IM groups or Web conversations and runs will appear here.

detail.errorHint: See the Steps tab below for the actual error.

runtime.name: Runtime
runtime.empty: No runtime snapshot was recorded for this run.

reason.unknownFailure: No clear failure reason was recorded.
reason.waitingForRuntime: Waiting for an available runtime to claim the run.
reason.runtimeNeedsAttention: The runtime state or heartbeat is unhealthy.
reason.running: Streaming execution is still in progress.
reason.completed: The output message was written back.

actions.connector: Check the Agent connector type, agent_kind, and daemon registration.
actions.timeout: Inspect runtime heartbeat and timeout logs; requeue if appropriate.
actions.runtime: Check daemon device, liveness, and latest heartbeat.
actions.model: Check model provider, model config, and opencode_json.
actions.inspect: Inspect events and audit records to locate the cause.
actions.requeueIfNeeded: Requeue after confirming the context is still valid.
actions.waitOrCancel: Wait for dispatch or cancel if no longer needed.
actions.inspectRuntime: Open runtime status and check heartbeat and availability.

runtimeActions.noSnapshot: Check whether this run is bound to an agent_daemon runtime.
runtimeActions.adminDisabled: Enable or repair the runtime before retrying.
runtimeActions.offline: Reconnect the daemon or switch to an available device.
runtimeActions.stale: Confirm the daemon is running and reporting heartbeats.

steps.title: Steps timeline
steps.empty: No persisted events yet. New runs will show token generation, tools, approvals, and completion here.

emptyFiltered.title: No matching runs
emptyFiltered.description: Adjust the filter or switch to the All tab to see every run.

emptyFiltered.title: No matching capabilities
emptyFiltered.description: Adjust the search or switch to All to see every capability.

dialog.title: Delete capability "{{name}}"
dialog.description: Deletion frees the name so it can be reused. Users who already installed this capability are not affected. This cannot be undone.

typeHelp.skill: Load a Skill package from a public GitHub repo when the Agent sandbox starts.

edit.title: Edit capability
edit.description: Type and version content cannot be changed here; add a new version to upgrade.
edit.credentialWarning: Changing the credential type can make existing user credentials stop matching; runs using this capability will ask users to set credentials again.

name.label: Capability name
name.help: 1-50 characters; avoid names already used in this workspace.

description.label: One-line description
description.placeholder: What can Agents do with this capability?

version.label: Version
version.help: Display version; 1.0.0 is a good first version.

gitRepoUrl.label: GitHub repository URL
gitRepoUrl.help: Public GitHub repositories only; private repos / SSH / GitLab are not supported yet.

gitRef.label: Version
gitRef.help: Prefer a pinned tag or commit SHA; main follows the latest code.

path.label: Subpath (optional)
path.help: Directory inside the repo; leave blank when the Skill package is at repo root.

notFound.title: This capability does not exist or was deleted
notFound.description: It may have been deleted, or this workspace cannot access it.

enabledAgents.title: Agents using this capability ({{count}})
enabledAgents.empty: No Agent is using this capability yet.

empty.title: This capability has no versions
empty.description: Add a version before Agents can enable it.

add.title: {{name}} — add version
add.description: A new version does not affect Agents already using older versions; they switch manually from Agent detail.
add.prefillFromLatest: Prefilled from the previous version ({{version}}). Edits become the new version on submit.
add.inlineSecretLostWarning: Inline secret(s) from the previous version ({{keys}}) are hidden. Re-enter the plaintext to keep them, or switch to a managed credential.

viewContent.title: View {{version}} content
viewContent.description: Read-only content used by this version at runtime.

deleteDialog.title: Delete {{version}} from "{{name}}"?
deleteDialog.description: This version will disappear from history.
deleteDialog.notAvailable: Deleting a single version isn't supported yet; to remove the entire capability use the Danger zone below.

danger.title: Danger zone
danger.description: Disabling or deleting affects Agents using this capability.
danger.deleteDisabled: Disable this capability on all Agents first (currently used by {{count}} Agent(s)).

disable.title: Disable "{{name}}"?
disable.description: After disabling: {{count}} Agent(s) using it will fail on their next run; runs already in flight fail after their current turn; credentials are kept and this can be re-enabled later.

enable.title: Enable "{{name}}"?
enable.description: After enabling, Agents can use this capability again on their next run.

delete.title: Permanently delete "{{name}}"?
delete.description: After deletion: this capability disappears from the workspace list, all versions are deleted together, and this cannot be undone. Current installations: {{count}}.

capabilities.deleteBlocked: Remove this capability from those Agents before deleting it.

errors.nameTooLong: Capability name must be at most 50 characters.
errors.plaintextSecretPattern: {{version}} content appears to contain a plaintext credential. Replace tokens / secrets with the ${PARSAR_CREDENTIAL} placeholder before publishing to the market.

toast.created: {{name}} was added. Enable it from Agent detail to use it.
toast.createdWithCredential: {{name}} was added. Next: enable it from Agent detail, or set {{kind}} once under My Credentials.

empty.title: No capabilities have been added to this workspace yet.
empty.descriptionAdmin: Capabilities live in the workspace capability pool. They are external tools (MCP) or loadable Skill packages that Agents in this workspace can enable as needed.
empty.descriptionMember: This workspace capability pool has no capabilities yet. Ask an owner / admin to add one.

loadError.title: Failed to load capabilities
loadError.hint: Make sure the server is running and the current workspace exists.

unreachable.title: Cannot reach the Parsar server
unreachable.description: The frontend cannot access the backend API (/api/v1/...). The server might be down, the port might be occupied, or the Vite proxy is misconfigured.
unreachable.hint: Check that the backend is running on port 18080: `make server` or `make dev`.

drawer.title: Capability market
drawer.description: Browse capabilities published by every workspace. Pick one, then enable it on a specific Agent from Agent detail.

empty.title: No capabilities are available in the market yet
empty.description: Capabilities published by other workspaces will appear here. You can publish your own capability from its detail page.

notFound.title: This market capability is not in the current workspace list
notFound.description: Only market capabilities enabled on at least one Agent appear here.

loadError.title: Failed to load market capability detail

enabledAgents.title: Agents using this capability ({{count}})
enabledAgents.empty: No Agent is using this capability right now.

deprecated.bannerTarget: The source workspace marked this capability deprecated. Agents already using it can keep using the pinned version, but cannot upgrade to a newer version.

marketStatus.title: Market status
marketStatus.deprecateTooltip: Once deprecated, the capability no longer appears in the picker when creating or editing agents. Existing agent bindings keep working as-is.
marketStatus.undeprecateTooltip: Restoring makes the capability appear in the picker again when creating or editing agents.

publish.title: Publish "{{name}}" to the capability market?
publish.description: After publishing, admins in other workspaces can see this capability in the market and enable the latest version on their Agents. Published versions are kept permanently for stability.

unpublish.title: Cancel publishing "{{name}}"?
unpublish.description: After this, the capability no longer appears in the market. The {{count}} workspace(s) that already added it are unaffected, but cannot upgrade to later versions.

deprecate.title: Mark "{{name}}" deprecated?
deprecate.description: This hides it from the market. The {{count}} workspace(s) that already added it keep using their pinned version, but will see a migration hint and cannot upgrade.

undeprecate.title: Clear deprecated state for "{{name}}"?
undeprecate.description: The capability appears in the market again, migration hints disappear, and existing workspaces can upgrade again.

uninstall.sectionDescription: Removal disables this capability on every Agent in the current workspace.
uninstall.title: Remove "{{name}}" from my workspace?
uninstall.description: After removal, this capability disappears from your capability list and will be disabled on {{count}} Agent(s):
uninstall.credentialNote: Existing my credentials are kept. Only the current workspace is affected.

envValue.inlineSecretPlaceholder: Paste a team-shared token; import saves it encrypted
envValue.inlineSecretNote: For a team-shared service token. After submit, the config keeps only a secret reference, not plaintext.
envValue.credentialRefNote: For personal tokens such as GitLab PATs. Runtime uses the value bound by the caller under My Credentials.

envHint.placeholderValue: This env placeholder needs a team shared secret or a personal credential.

dialog.description: Paste a third-party MCP config (JSON / TOML) to parse and preview it. Ordinary env values are imported as-is; only env values that start with $ need a credential source.
dialog.descriptionSkill: Paste a SKILL.md or upload a zip bundle. The Skill's name and description come from its frontmatter; the body is injected into the model as instruction.
dialog.descriptionPlugin: Upload a packaged .claude-plugin zip; the server validates the manifest and parses it.
dialog.descriptionPlaceholder: One sentence about what this capability does — Claude uses it to decide when to invoke.

skill.dropHint: Drag or click to upload a .zip (SKILL.md + references / scripts)
skill.zipHelp: The zip must include a SKILL.md (at the root or one subdirectory deep). references/ and scripts/ folders are imported alongside it.

errors.tooLarge: File exceeds 8 MiB — the server will reject it

page.title: Agents
page.description: Manage your digital teammates: identity, capabilities, default model, where they run.

visibility.workspaceHint: Only members of this workspace can invoke (incl. via Feishu bot)
visibility.tenantHint: Any registered Parsar user can invoke
visibility.publicHint: Any Feishu user can invoke (incl. those not registered)
visibility.downgradeWarnTitle: Tightening visibility will lock out external / cross-tenant users immediately
visibility.downgradeWarnBody: Non-workspace / non-tenant users currently chatting with this Agent will start being rejected right away. Historical conversations are preserved. Switch to {{to}}?

feishuConnector.title: Feishu Bot binding
feishuConnector.description: Bind this Agent to a Feishu self-built app so users can @mention it in chats and groups. Credentials (App Secret / Verification Token / Encrypt Key) must live in Secrets — this form only stores references.

provision.title: Create Bot by QR
provision.subtitle: Admin consent stores credentials automatically
provision.qrAlt: QR code for Feishu Bot creation

enabled.label: Enable Feishu Bot binding
enabled.hint: When off, this Bot app no longer receives inbound messages and outbound messages are not sent

appId.label: App ID
appId.hint: The Feishu self-built app's App ID, e.g. cli_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

entryMode.label: Bot entry
entryMode.hint: The default Bot is the team entry point and does not require a separate Feishu app for this Agent. Choosing a dedicated Bot removes this Agent from the default Bot list.

appSecret.label: App Secret
appSecret.hint: Saved as an encrypted Secret on save; Agent config stores only the reference.
appSecret.savedHint: Saved already. Leave blank to keep the current value; enter a new value to rotate it.

verificationToken.label: Verification Token
verificationToken.hint: Token from Feishu event-subscription settings. Saved as an encrypted Secret on save.
verificationToken.savedHint: Saved already. Leave blank to keep the current value; enter a new value to rotate it.

encryptKey.label: Encrypt Key (optional)
encryptKey.hint: Only fill this when Feishu event encryption is enabled. Saved as an encrypted Secret on save.
encryptKey.savedHint: Saved already. Leave blank to keep the current value; enter a new value to rotate it.

botOpenId.label: Bot Open ID (optional)
botOpenId.hint: The bot's own open_id, used to dedup messages the bot posts itself

errors.appIdInUse: This App ID is already bound to another active Agent in this instance. Pick a different App ID, or disable the other Agent's Feishu binding first.
errors.incomplete: When enabled, App ID / App Secret / Verification Token are all required.

runtime.label: Runtime
runtime.localHint: The agent runs directly in this machine's process, sharing your file system.
runtime.sandboxHint: The agent runs in an isolated cloud sandbox. Doesn't touch your local files.

loadError.title: Failed to load agents
loadError.hint: Make sure the server is running and the current workspace exists.

unreachable.title: Cannot reach the Parsar server
unreachable.description: The frontend cannot access the backend API (/api/v1/...). The server might be down, the port might be occupied, or the Vite proxy is misconfigured.
unreachable.hint: Check that the backend is running on port 18080: `make server` or `make dev`.

mockBanner.title: Choose a data scope
mockBanner.hint: Choose the matching Workspace in the header.

empty.title: No agents yet
empty.description: Create the first Agent so it can work alongside the team in Feishu / Slack / Web.

current.title: Current
current.subtitle: in-flight work
current.empty: This agent has no task running right now.

metrics.title: Last 30 days
metrics.subtitle: performance
metrics.empty: No completed runs in the last 30 days.

capabilities.title: Capabilities
capabilities.empty: No capability enabled yet. Click "+ Add" to install one from the workspace.

sections.unregistered: Capabilities not registered in this workspace

empty.title: No capabilities are enabled for this Agent yet.
empty.description: The Agent can only use general chat for now. Pick one from Available in workspace below.

capabilities.emptyEnabled: No capabilities are enabled for this Agent yet. Pick one from Available in workspace below.
capabilities.emptyAvailable: No more workspace capabilities are available.
capabilities.credentialsLoadError: Could not load my credential status; actions remain visible and the backend will still enforce permissions.

credential.missing: Requires {{kind}} · ✗ not set — calls will fail

enableDialog.title: Enable "{{cap}}" on "{{agent}}"
enableDialog.description: Once enabled, this Agent can call this capability during runs.
enableDialog.noCredential: This capability does not need an external account.
enableDialog.credentialReady: ✓ Found your {{kind}} credential. It can be used immediately after enabling.
enableDialog.credentialMissing: ✗ This capability requires {{kind}}, which you have not set yet.
enableDialog.ackMissingCredential: I understand this capability needs a credential to work, and it will show as "Credential missing" after enabling

switchDialog.title: Switch "{{cap}}" version
switchDialog.description: The Agent uses the new version on its next run; in-flight runs are unchanged.
switchDialog.notice: Only this Agent ({{agent}}) is affected. Other Agents using this capability are unchanged. Takes effect on the next run.

removeDialog.title: Remove "{{cap}}" from "{{agent}}"?
removeDialog.description: Removal takes effect on the next run; existing my credentials are not deleted.
removeDialog.impactRun: This Agent can no longer call this capability.
removeDialog.impactCapability: The capability stays registered in the workspace and can be re-enabled later.
removeDialog.impactCredential: Existing my credentials are not deleted.

unregistered.description: If the capability you need is not registered in the workspace, contact an owner / admin to add it.
unregistered.memberHint: Contact an owner / admin if you need a new capability.

deletedCapability.title: This capability was deleted from the workspace
deletedCapability.description: Runs will fail. Ask an admin to register it again or remove it here.

toast.enabledMissingCredential: "{{cap}}" is enabled, but you must set {{kind}} in My Credentials before it can work.
toast.switched: "{{cap}}" switched to v{{version}}. Takes effect on the next run.
toast.removed: Removed "{{cap}}" from "{{agent}}". Takes effect on the next run.
toast.upgraded: "{{cap}}" upgraded to v{{version}}. Takes effect on the next run.

marketplace.deprecatedBanner: The source workspace marked this capability deprecated. This Agent can keep using pinned v{{version}}, but cannot upgrade.
marketplace.upgradeBlocked: Source capability is deprecated; upgrade is unavailable.

runs.hint: Agent detail shows a run summary; for the full table go to Runs filtered by agent.

sandbox.title: Persistent Sandbox
sandbox.notLiveHint: Sandbox is no longer live. Kill and Rebuild are disabled — the next prompt will lift a fresh sandbox automatically.

tabBadge.live: A live sandbox is bound to this agent
tabBadge.terminal: Sandbox killed or terminal — the next prompt will lift a new one

empty.title: No active sandbox
empty.description: No long-lived sandbox is bound to this agent right now. The next prompt will lift a fresh one (or the runtime is in local mode and never spawns one).

local.title: This Agent runs locally
local.description: Local mode does not need a long-lived sandbox. Switch to Sandbox to show status, Kill, and Rebuild actions here.

sandbox.configHint: If Sandbox is selected, make sure this workspace has a sandbox runtime configured; otherwise startup will fail.
sandbox.provisioningHint: Sandbox is being provisioned. A warm start usually takes 10-30 seconds; the first local Docker startup can take longer while the sandbox image is pulled.

preparing.title: Preparing sandbox
preparing.body: Starting the sandbox and waiting for parsar-daemon to pair.
preparing.coldStartBody: A warm start usually takes 10-30 seconds. The first local Docker startup can take longer while the sandbox image is pulled.

steps.prepareImageDetail: Checking the sandbox image and startup settings.
steps.pullImageDetail: If this image is not cached locally, Docker may be downloading it now.
steps.pullImageSlowDetail: Still waiting. First pull can take several minutes on a slow network.
steps.startContainerDetail: Create the isolated sandbox container after the image is ready.
steps.pairDaemonDetail: Wait for parsar-daemon inside the sandbox to connect back.

startupTimedOut.title: Sandbox startup timed out
startupTimedOut.body: The sandbox did not pair before the startup token expired. This often happens when the first local Docker startup is still pulling the sandbox image.

kill.title: Kill this sandbox?
kill.description: The long-lived E2B sandbox bound to this agent will be torn down immediately. In-flight work in this sandbox is lost; the next prompt will lift a fresh sandbox from the base template (10-20s cold start). If you recently changed the agent's sandbox size, the next cold start picks up the new size.

rebuild.title: Rebuild this sandbox?
rebuild.description: The current sandbox is torn down so the next prompt re-spawns from a clean base template. Use this when the sandbox has accumulated bad state (cached dependencies, stale clone, etc.). In-flight work is lost.

emptyFiltered.title: No matching agents
emptyFiltered.description: Adjust the keyword or switch to the All tab to see every agent.

placeholders.name: e.g. Data analyst assistant
placeholders.description: One-line description of what this Agent is good at
placeholders.systemPrompt: Leave blank to use the default assistant prompt

errors.nameConflict: An Agent with this name already exists. Pick another name.
errors.slugConflict: The internal identifier is already taken. Pick another name.
errors.generatedIdConflict: This name generates an internal identifier that is already taken. Pick another name.
errors.generatedIdInvalid: Name needs at least one letter, number, or Chinese character.
errors.workDirAbsolute: Working directory must be an absolute path (starting with /).

sandboxSize.hint: Changes take effect after the current sandbox is released.

sandboxImage.hint: The server pulls this container image when the Agent's sandbox starts for the first time.

emptyModel.title: No usable Model yet
emptyModel.description: Go to Models to onboard one. The fields you typed here will be carried over so you can come back.

emptyConnector.title: No usable Connector yet
emptyConnector.description: Configure a Connector first so the Agent knows where to run.

form.runtimeHint: Runtime is part of an agent's identity and cannot be changed after creation. To switch, recreate the agent. Cloud Sandbox requires the deployment to have cloud runtime enabled; open-source/self-hosted deployments may also need a workspace runtime credential.
form.runtimeLocked: Runtime is set at creation and cannot be changed
form.noTagsAdmin: This workspace has no reusable capability tags yet.
form.noTagsMember: This workspace has no reusable capability tags yet.
form.deprecatedCapabilityTooltip: This capability has been deprecated. Existing bindings keep working; once you uncheck it you cannot re-add it.

versionPicker.localHint: Pick latest to auto-follow re-uploads. Pick a specific version to pin.
versionPicker.marketplaceHint: Marketplace: latest follows the publisher's newest release, which may include breaking changes.

runtime.title: Runtime
runtime.summary: Where the agent runs and which model it uses

capabilities.title: Capabilities
capabilities.summary: Skills, MCPs and plugins enabled by default

devicePicker.hint: Pick a connected local daemon. Cloud isolation does not need a device; Parsar lazy-starts a sandbox daemon on first run.

workDir.hintLocal: Directory the agent runs in on your local device. Leave blank to use a per-conversation scratch dir. Created if it does not exist.
workDir.hintSandbox: Directory inside the sandbox container (e.g. /workspace/my-app). Leave blank to use a per-conversation scratch dir. Created if it does not exist.

pendingCapability.banner: You are preparing to add "{{name}}" (from {{source}}). Choose an Agent to enable it.
pendingCapability.detailBanner: You are adding "{{name}}" (from {{source}}). Use Enable in the Available section below to finish.
pendingCapability.loading: Loading the capability you are preparing to add…

listActions.deleteConfirmDescription: After deletion, this Agent is no longer available and will not receive new run requests. Historical conversations, runs, and audit records are kept.

sandbox.title: Cloud isolation
sandbox.description: Parsar prepares an isolated sandbox in ~10s.
sandbox.poolEmpty: Sandbox pool is empty — cold start required after creation

localDevice.title: Local device
localDevice.description: Use a paired local device for local code.

external.title: External Agent
external.description: Route to a self-managed HTTP Agent service.

claudeCode.title: Claude Code
claudeCode.description: Run Claude Code CLI through parsar-daemon.

codex.title: Codex
codex.description: Run OpenAI Codex (codex-rs) via the app-server protocol.

pi.title: Pi
pi.description: Run the pi coding agent CLI through parsar-daemon.

opencode.title: OpenCode
opencode.description: Run OpenCode CLI through parsar-daemon for local or sandbox daemon workflows.

page.title: Connectors
page.description: How Agents actually run. A Connector is the integration layer — one connector can carry multiple Agents.

connectors.aggregateHint: This view aggregates connector types in use by Agents in the current workspace. Connectors don't have their own registry table by design (anti-overengineering: connector identity lives directly on each Agent's connector_type field).

loadError.title: Failed to load connectors
loadError.hint: Make sure the server is running and the current workspace exists.

unreachable.title: Cannot reach the Parsar server
unreachable.description: The frontend cannot access the backend API (/api/v1/...). The server might be down, the port might be occupied, or the Vite proxy is misconfigured.
unreachable.hint: Check that the backend is running on port 18080: `make server` or `make dev`.

mockBanner.title: Choose a data scope
mockBanner.hint: Choose the matching Workspace in the header.

empty.title: No Agents in this workspace use any connector yet
empty.description: Create an Agent in Agents and set its connector_type — this view aggregates automatically.

notFound.title: Connector type not found
notFound.description: It may have been removed, or no Agent in the current workspace uses it.

page.title: Credentials
page.description: Review, rotate, and disable API keys and OAuth credentials Parsar uses to access external services. Credentials are usually created automatically by product flows (Models, Runtime, Connections).

sections.modelKeysDescription: Usually saved and bound automatically when creating Providers from Models.
sections.runtimeKeysDescription: Used by cloud runtime providers such as E2B.

usage.modelProvider: Used by a model Provider; normally bound from Models.
usage.feishu: Used by Feishu entry points or Agent bots.
usage.custom: Custom API key; bind it from the relevant capability or configuration.

create.title: Add API Key
create.description: Fallback entry. Prefer creating model, runtime, and OAuth credentials from their product flows. API keys are encrypted by the server.

disable.title: Disable "{{name}}"?
disable.description: Models, capabilities, or runtimes depending on it may stop working. The record is kept.

empty.modelKeys: No model API keys yet. They are usually created from the Models page.
empty.runtimeKeys: No runtime credentials yet. Configure one when enabling cloud runtime.

load.title: Failed to load credentials
load.hint: Make sure the server is running and the current workspace exists.

unreachable.title: Cannot reach Parsar server
unreachable.description: The frontend cannot reach the backend API.
unreachable.hint: Check that the backend is running on port 18080.

pendingRequests.confirmRejectBody: Reject the join request from "{{name}}"? They can submit a new request anytime.

search.typeToSearch: Type a name or email to search platform users

invite.title: Invite a new teammate
invite.description: Creates a secure invitation link for this email and role. The link expires after 72 hours.
invite.namePlaceholder: Optional — defaults to the email prefix
invite.resultBody: Send this invitation link to {{email}}. It expires in 72 hours.
invite.existingUserNotice: If this email already belongs to a Parsar user, accepting the invitation adds their existing account to the workspace.

remove.title: Remove "{{name}}"?
remove.description: Soft-deletes the user's workspace membership. The user record itself is preserved and can be re-added later.

ws.title: No workspace members yet
ws.description: Click "Add member" in the top-right to add the first user.

load.title: Failed to load members
load.hint: Make sure the server is running and the workspace exists.

unreachable.title: Cannot reach Parsar server
unreachable.description: The frontend can't reach the backend API (/api/v1/...). The server may be down, the port occupied, or the Vite proxy misconfigured.
unreachable.hint: Make sure the backend is up on port 18080: `make server` or `make dev`.

mockBanner.title: {{scope}}
mockBanner.ws: Choose a Workspace in the header first.

workspace.title: Basic settings
workspace.description: Only workspace identity settings with a clear current effect live here.
workspace.name: Workspace name
workspace.nameHint: Current workspace display name; rename it from the workspace switcher in the header.
workspace.runtimeRootHint: Config, logs, state, and cache live here instead of the repository root.

authentication.title: Authentication
authentication.description: Read-only login provider status from deployment configuration. Secrets stay in env or your secret manager.
authentication.error: Could not load auth provider status.

gatewayDefaults.title: Gateway defaults
gatewayDefaults.description: Defaults applied when a new gateway is wired up. Existing gateways aren't affected.
gatewayDefaults.defaultVisibilityHint: Who can see new conversations from this gateway by default.
gatewayDefaults.dedupWindowHint: Same external event_id is processed only once within this window.
gatewayDefaults.replayProtectionHint: Reject webhook events with stale timestamps to mitigate replay attacks.

pat.title: Personal Access Tokens
pat.description: Policy for tokens members issue manually.
pat.allowExpandHint: Off means only admins can grant new scopes to a PAT.

oidc.title: OIDC SSO
oidc.description: Connect to your enterprise IdP (Okta / Azure AD / Auth0).
oidc.note: OIDC is optional; without it, members log in via local accounts.

capability.title: Capability management
capability.description: Workspace runtime capability management has moved to Agent Management → Capabilities.
capability.migratedHint: Capability management has moved to Agent Management → Capabilities.
capability.requireApprovalHighHint: filesystem.write, secret.read, external_api.call etc. cannot be self-approved by Agents.

workingDir.title: Working directory policy
workingDir.description: Boundaries the Agent runtime is allowed to touch.
workingDir.absolutePathsOnlyHint: Reject relative paths to avoid cwd drift writing to the wrong place.

policy.title: Current runtime policy
policy.description: These are the rules that currently take effect or have a clear product boundary. Backend-less controls are no longer shown as editable settings.

workdirs.title: Working directory input
workdirs.body: User-provided working directories must be absolute paths or start with ~/. Relative paths are rejected.

runtimeState.title: Runtime state location
runtimeState.body: Config, logs, state, and cache must be written under the Parsar runtime root, not the current repository.

agentRuntime.title: Agent execution mode
agentRuntime.body: Runtime is selected explicitly when an Agent is created and is immutable afterward; workspace settings no longer store a default execution mode.

capabilities.title: Capability boundary
capabilities.body: Tool permissions, capability toggles, and credentials belong in Capabilities and Connectors instead of fake Settings controls.

dataRetention.title: Data retention
dataRetention.description: How long run history and audit logs are kept.

experimental.title: Experimental features
experimental.description: Unstable capabilities. Evaluate risk before enabling.
experimental.agentToAgentHint: Agents can @-mention each other. Chain depth controlled per workspace.
experimental.memoryProposalHint: Agent memory writes need human approval first.

danger.title: Danger zone
danger.deleteWorkspaceHint: Permanently deletes all conversations, runs, artifacts, and audit logs. Cannot be undone.

page.title: Audit log
page.description: Every traceable action in Parsar. Identity, configuration, runtime, approval, and data-access events are grouped under 5 source categories, newest first.

filters.targetTypeNone: No target types in this source

search.placeholder: Search by event / actor / target ID

loadError.title: Failed to load audit records
loadError.hint: Make sure the server is running and the current workspace exists.

unreachable.title: Cannot reach the Parsar server
unreachable.description: The frontend cannot access the backend API (/api/v1/...). The server might be down, the port might be occupied, or the Vite proxy is misconfigured.
unreachable.hint: Check that the backend is running on port 18080: `make server` or `make dev`.

mockBanner.title: Choose a data scope
mockBanner.hint: Choose the matching Workspace in the header.

empty.title: No audit records
empty.description: This workspace has no audit events yet. Wait for new activity.
empty.filteredDescription: No records match the current filters. Switch tabs, change target type, or clear the search term.

empty.title: No audit events for this resource
empty.description: This resource has not produced any audit records yet.

loadError.title: Failed to load usage data
loadError.hint: Make sure the server is running and the current workspace exists.

unreachable.title: Cannot reach the Parsar server
unreachable.description: The frontend cannot access the backend API (/api/v1/...). The server might be down, the port might be occupied, or the Vite proxy is misconfigured.
unreachable.hint: Check that the backend is running on port 18080: `make server` or `make dev`.

mockBanner.title: Choose a data scope
mockBanner.hint: Choose the matching Workspace in the header.

empty.title: No usage data yet
empty.description: Usage rows are written automatically after agents run.

page.title: My Credentials
page.description: Set up the external accounts you use across all workspaces. Each credential type is set once and shared across workspaces; only you can see it, and collaborators on the same Agent use their own credentials.

returnBanner.description: After finishing setup, return to where you came from.

search.placeholder: Search by type or display name

empty.title: You have not added any credentials yet
empty.description: When a capability you enable, or a teammate's Agent acting for you, needs GitHub, Slack, a database, or another external account, add it here once and reuse it across workspaces.

emptyFiltered.title: No matching credentials
emptyFiltered.description: Try another search term, or clear the search to see every credential.

load.title: Failed to load credentials
load.hint: Check that the server is running and the current user is signed in.

unreachable.title: Cannot reach Parsar server
unreachable.description: The frontend cannot reach the backend API (/api/v1/...). The server may be down, the port may be occupied, or the Vite proxy may be misconfigured.
unreachable.hint: Check that the backend is listening on port 18080: `make server` or `make dev`.

dialog.description: Only you can see this credential. Parsar stores it encrypted.
dialog.tokenSet: Credential value is set; Parsar will not display the plaintext again.

fields.kindHint: Need another type? Contact an owner or admin. Custom types are planned for a later version.
fields.displayNameHint: Only you see this. It helps you recognize the credential and does not affect Agent calls.
fields.valueHint: Used only while saving the encrypted value; it will not be shown again.

delete.title: Delete the {{kind}} credential?
delete.description: After deletion, capabilities that need {{kind}} can no longer be triggered by you. Agents with those capabilities enabled will fail the next time you start a conversation and ask you to set the credential again. The encrypted value is destroyed and cannot be recovered.
delete.impactTitle: Capabilities currently using this credential type
delete.noImpact: No capability currently uses this credential type.
delete.hasImpact: {{count}} capabilities across {{workspaceCount}} workspaces use this credential type.

security.title: How credentials are used
security.encrypted: Credential values are encrypted at rest, and Parsar never displays plaintext to anyone.
security.crossWorkspace: Credentials are user-level and shared across workspaces, so you do not need to set the same type repeatedly.
security.sandbox: When an Agent calls a capability that needs a credential, v0.2 injects your credential when you initiate the conversation; calls by others in that same conversation also use your credential. A later version will inject credentials per action requester.

credentialsPage.title: Credentials
credentialsPage.description: Credentials come in two kinds. "Mine" are your external accounts (GitHub, Notion, etc.) — shared across workspaces, visible only to you. "Org-shared" are workspace-admin credentials (model API keys, runtime keys, …).

banner.title: {{count}} credential(s) still needed
banner.description: Agents, capabilities, or models in your workspaces require these. The runtime will block calls until they're set — adding them now avoids interruptions later.

empty.title: You have not added any credentials yet
empty.description: When a capability needs an external account (GitHub, Slack, a database, …), add it here once and reuse it across every workspace you belong to.

org.scopeNote: Manages org-shared service credentials (model API keys, runtime keys, …). Visible and editable to workspace admins only.

create.title: Add an org-shared credential
create.description: Used for runtime (e.g. E2B) or custom service API keys. Model API keys are saved automatically when you create a Provider on the Models page.

credentialCheck.sourcePersonal: Each caller uses their own token
credentialCheck.sourceSharedNew: Create a new shared secret (paste once)
credentialCheck.personalDisabledHint: Public agents cannot use personal credentials (callers may not be platform users)
credentialCheck.personalYouHaveIt: You have configured this ✓ (other callers must configure their own)
credentialCheck.personalYouMissing: You haven't configured this yet — calls will be disabled
credentialCheck.sharedNoneAvailable: No shared secrets available in this workspace
credentialCheck.modelBindingNew: Create a new shared API key (paste once)

local.title: Local runtime
local.placeholder: Local runtime is always available · Agent instance summary is in progress.

sandbox.title: Cloud-isolated runtime
sandbox.healthy: Credential ready · {{count}} Agent(s) configured to run in the cloud.
sandbox.notConfigured: No workspace credential registered yet — sandbox-mode Agents cannot start.
sandbox.misconfigured: Credential present but unreachable. Reset it or contact the operator.
sandbox.unreachable: Status unavailable — backend did not respond.

credential.title: E2B API Key
credential.description: Agents using Cloud Sandbox use this key. Once saved, only the masked form is shown.

save.description: Once saved, only the masked form is shown; plaintext is never displayed.

delete.title: Delete E2B API Key?
delete.description: Agents using Cloud Sandbox cannot start until a new key is entered.

status.cloudReadyHint: Agents in this workspace can run inside isolated cloud environments.
status.cloudReadyManaged: Runtime is managed by Parsar — no setup required.
status.cloudReadyOps: Cloud runtime ready · configured by your administrator. Contact ops to adjust quotas.
status.cloudOff: No cloud credential registered for this workspace
status.cloudOffHint: Agents can still run locally or in external environments. To use cloud isolation, register a credential in the Workspace credential card below.
status.cloudMisconfigured: Cloud runtime configuration is incomplete — check the workspace credential or contact the operator.
status.unreachable: Cannot reach the Parsar backend; runtime status is unknown.
status.cloudRunnerUnavailableHint: This deployment has not enabled Cloud Sandbox yet. Contact the deployment administrator.

newDeployment.title: Waiting for the first agent to spin up a cloud runtime
newDeployment.description: Any Agent switched to "cloud" runtime mode will boot an isolated environment on its next message and appear here.
newDeployment.tryCta: Want to verify cloud runtime is wired before any Agent uses it? Click the button below — a test sandbox runs the health checks and is auto-reclaimed. It does not affect any Agent.

localOss.title: No cloud credential registered for this workspace
localOss.description: Agents can still run locally or externally. To run an Agent inside an isolated cloud environment, register the sandbox provider's API key in the Workspace credential card above.
localOss.afterSnippet: Once registered, every runtime=sandbox Agent in this workspace shares the credential. Full setup at
localOss.e2bSignup: No E2B account yet? Create one at E2B

localManaged.title: Cloud runtime is not enabled
localManaged.description: This workspace currently has no cloud runtime quota. Contact the Parsar team or your workspace owner to enable it.
localManaged.fallback: Agents can still run locally / externally — open Agent detail → Runtime tab and pick any option other than "Follow workspace".

localSelfhost.title: No workspace runtime credential yet
localSelfhost.description: This private deployment is running in local mode. Workspace admins can register a cloud sandbox credential in the "Workspace credential" card above; if your company centrally manages credentials, ask ops or IT for help.
localSelfhost.afterSnippet: Until a credential is registered, Agents only run locally or in external environments.

misconfiguredOss.title: Cloud runtime configuration is incomplete
misconfiguredOss.description: The cloud runtime credential saved for this workspace cannot connect. Try:
misconfiguredOss.step1: Verify the workspace E2B credential is still valid;
misconfiguredOss.step2: Search server logs for entries starting with sandbox-runner / e2b-client;
misconfiguredOss.step3: After fixing the credential, click [ 🔁 Recheck cloud reachability ] or [ Edit E2B credential ] in the top-right to verify.

misconfiguredManaged.title: Cloud runtime is temporarily unavailable
misconfiguredManaged.description: We have received the runtime alert for this workspace and the Parsar team is investigating. Affected Agents will automatically fall back to local or external mode.

misconfiguredSelfhost.title: Cloud runtime is misconfigured
misconfiguredSelfhost.description: The backend detected that cloud runtime credentials cannot connect. Ask your company's ops team to check the deployment logs:
misconfiguredSelfhost.step1: Look for sandbox-runner / e2b-client entries in the server log;
misconfiguredSelfhost.step2: Verify the egress firewall allows the Parsar server to reach the E2B endpoint.

errors.bulkKillPartial: Some runtime instances could not be reclaimed

confirmBulkKill.title: Reclaim {{count}} runtime instance(s)?
confirmBulkKill.description: Each selected long-lived instance is torn down immediately. In-flight work is lost; the next message for each Agent triggers a fresh cold start (10-20s each).

detail.placeholderDescription: Full detail view (connectivity test, overview, technical details) lands in a follow-up PR.
detail.placeholderBody: Detail page in progress — Agent ID: {{id}}
detail.title: Runtime instance · {{agentName}}
detail.subtitle: Isolated cloud environment · started lazily on the Agent's next message; auto-reclaimed past the idle threshold.

managedBoundary.title: Management boundary
managedBoundary.description: Parsar can only govern behavior it registers and delivers. Anything placed or run manually in the sandbox is outside direct audit coverage.

managed.actionCredentials: Action credentials injected for capability calls

unmanaged.manualSsh: Commands run manually after SSH into the sandbox
unmanaged.directApi: Agent calls to external APIs through shell or curl

connectivity.emptyHint: No connectivity test has been run yet. Click [ Connectivity test ] in the header to try one.
connectivity.emptyHintSecondary: (The test is read-only and does not affect Agent work.)

nextSteps.credInvalid: Ask your administrator to check cloud runtime credentials.
nextSteps.quotaExceeded: Ask your administrator to raise the quota or reclaim unused instances.
nextSteps.unreachable: Retry shortly; rebuild this instance if it keeps failing.
nextSteps.runtimeDown: Try Rebuild on this instance, then retry the connectivity test.
nextSteps.promptTimeout: Try Rebuild on this instance; ask your administrator if it persists.
nextSteps.unknown: Ask your administrator to inspect server logs.

connectivity.notRunAfter: Subsequent checks were not run (prerequisite failed).

sandbox.title: Cloud Sandbox
sandbox.description: Run Agents in isolated cloud environments for stronger isolation and credential governance.

localDevice.title: Local Device
localDevice.description: Connect team-owned machines to Parsar so Agents can use local CLIs, workdirs, and BYO credentials.

external.title: External Agent
external.description: Attach an existing Agent service to Parsar from the Agent configuration.

agent_daemon.title: Local Device
agent_daemon.description: Connect team-owned machines to Parsar so Agents can use local CLIs, workdirs, and BYO credentials.

deploymentOff.label: Not enabled
deploymentOff.body: Cloud Sandbox is not enabled for this deployment yet. No workspace setup is needed until it is enabled.
deploymentOff.bodyWithCredential: The E2B API Key is saved, but Cloud Sandbox is not enabled for this deployment yet.

notConfigured.label: Not configured
notConfigured.body: Enter an E2B API Key before Agents can use Cloud Sandbox.

ready.label: Ready
ready.body: Cloud Sandbox is ready. {{count}} Agent(s) currently use Cloud Sandbox.

error.label: Error
error.body: Cloud Sandbox is temporarily unavailable. Check workspace configuration and validate again.
error.managedBody: Cloud Sandbox is managed by the deployment but is temporarily unavailable. Contact the deployment administrator to check the sandbox service.
error.bodyWithCredential: {{value}} is saved, but connection validation did not pass. Re-enter the E2B API Key and validate again.

unknown.label: Unknown
unknown.body: Runtime status is temporarily unavailable. Refresh and try again.

managed.title: Managed by organization
managed.body: This workspace does not need its own E2B API Key.

instances.title: Active Cloud Sandboxes
instances.emptyBody: Agents using Cloud Sandbox start an isolated environment on their next message and appear here.

daemonRuntimes.title: Sandbox Daemons
daemonRuntimes.description: parsar-daemon processes running inside Cloud Sandboxes. They belong to Cloud Sandbox, not Local Device.

status.preparingDetail: Starting the sandbox and waiting for parsar-daemon to pair. First local Docker startup may include pulling the sandbox image.
status.timedOutDetail: The sandbox did not pair before the startup token expired. Retry provisioning from the Agent detail.

external.body: External Agents do not need workspace-level setup. Create or edit an Agent, choose External Agent, then fill in its endpoint and auth method.

agentDaemon.intro: Local devices connect back to Parsar through parsar-daemon so Agents can use Claude Code / OpenCode CLIs on that machine.
agentDaemon.empty: No local device is connected yet. Generate a connection command from the button on the right.

detail.disabled: Scheduling is paused by an admin; the daemon can still report heartbeats.
detail.pendingPairing: Waiting for parsar-daemon connect to finish pairing.
detail.offlineNoSnapshot: No capability snapshot yet; confirm parsar-daemon is connected on the device.
detail.offlineWithSnapshot: Heartbeat is interrupted; run parsar-daemon status or parsar-daemon logs on the device.
detail.error: The daemon reported an error; run parsar-daemon logs on the device.
detail.onlineNoCli: WebSocket is online, but no Agent CLI is available on this machine.
detail.onlineIdle: Online and waiting for new requests.

pair.title: Connect device
pair.description: Generate a one-time token, then run parsar-daemon connect on the target machine. The daemon connects back to Parsar over WebSocket.
pair.safetyOutbound: The machine connects outbound; no inbound port is required
pair.safetyOnce: The token is shown only once, so copy it right after generation
pair.success: Connection command generated. Run this command on the target machine:

workspace.title: Choose a Workspace to view {{resource}}
workspace.description: This data is managed by Workspace. Choose a Workspace in the header first.

providers.github: For GitHub PR, issue, and repository capabilities.
providers.slack: For Slack messages, channels, and workflow capabilities.
providers.feishu: For Feishu apps, message delivery, and event verification.

oauth.title: Third-party accounts
oauth.empty: No third-party accounts are available to connect yet.

model.title: Model API keys
model.empty: No model API key types are available yet.

createKind.title: Add a model API key type
createKind.codeHint: Lowercase letters, digits, underscores; cannot be changed later
createKind.errorGeneric: Failed to create, please try again later

connector.adminOnly: Only workspace owners and admins can edit connector settings.

platformList.title: Messaging platforms
platformList.description: Pick a platform, fill its credentials, then enable it when the config is complete.

feishu.title: Feishu connector
feishu.description: Bind one Feishu bot for this workspace. All agents reachable from this workspace share this bot.

docLink.label: Open the Feishu Open Platform to create / manage your bot

enabled.label: Enabled
enabled.hint: Turn on to route Feishu messages through this bot.

appId.label: App ID
appId.hint: Credentials & Basic Info page → App ID (starts with cli_).

appSecret.label: App secret
appSecret.hint: Credentials & Basic Info page → App Secret. Stored encrypted.
appSecret.savedHint: A saved app secret is in place (Credentials & Basic Info → App Secret). Enter a new value to replace it.

verificationToken.label: Verification token
verificationToken.hint: Event Subscriptions page → Verification Token. Required for webhook mode. Stored encrypted.
verificationToken.savedHint: A saved verification token is in place (Event Subscriptions → Verification Token). Enter a new value to replace it.

encryptKey.label: Encrypt key
encryptKey.hint: Event Subscriptions page → Encrypt Key. Optional; decrypts event payloads. Stored encrypted.
encryptKey.savedHint: A saved encrypt key is in place (Event Subscriptions → Encrypt Key). Enter a new value to replace it.

botOpenId.label: Bot open ID (optional)
botOpenId.hint: Leave blank — the server auto-fetches it from the app credentials (bot/v3/info) on save. To set it manually, use the bot's open_id (ou_…). Used to recognize @mentions and skip self-sent messages.

eventMode.label: Event mode
eventMode.hint: WebSocket opens a long-lived connection (works on localhost); webhook receives HTTP callbacks.

errors.appIdInUse: This Feishu app ID is already bound to another workspace.
errors.incomplete: The Feishu connector is incomplete. Fill in all required fields.
errors.botOpenIdResolveFailed: Couldn't auto-fetch the bot open_id. Check the App ID / App Secret, or enter it manually.
errors.generic: Failed to save the Feishu connector. Please try again.

slack.title: Slack connector
slack.description: Bind one Slack bot for this workspace. All agents reachable from this workspace share this bot.

docLink.label: Create a Slack app at api.slack.com/apps

enabled.label: Enabled
enabled.hint: Turn on to route Slack messages through this bot.

appId.label: App ID
appId.hint: Basic Information page → App Credentials → App ID (starts with A).

botToken.label: Bot token
botToken.hint: OAuth & Permissions tab → Bot User OAuth Token (xoxb-…). Install the app to your workspace first. Stored encrypted.
botToken.savedHint: A saved bot token is in place (OAuth & Permissions → Bot User OAuth Token). Enter a new xoxb token to replace it.

appToken.label: App-level token
appToken.hint: Basic Information → App-Level Tokens → Generate Token with the connections:write scope (xapp-…). Required for socket mode. Stored encrypted.
appToken.savedHint: A saved app-level token is in place (Basic Information → App-Level Tokens). Enter a new xapp token to replace it.

signingSecret.label: Signing secret
signingSecret.hint: Basic Information → App Credentials → Signing Secret. Required for events mode. Stored encrypted.
signingSecret.savedHint: A saved signing secret is in place (Basic Information → App Credentials → Signing Secret). Enter a new value to replace it.

eventMode.label: Event mode
eventMode.hint: Socket opens a long-lived connection (works on localhost, needs an xapp token); events receives HTTP callbacks (needs a signing secret).

errors.appIdInUse: This Slack app ID is already bound to another workspace.
errors.incomplete: The Slack connector is incomplete. Fill in all required fields.
errors.generic: Failed to save the Slack connector. Please try again.

discord.title: Discord connector
discord.description: Bind one Discord bot for this workspace. All agents reachable from this workspace share this bot.

docLink.label: Create a Discord app in the Developer Portal

enabled.label: Enabled
enabled.hint: Turn on to route Discord messages through this bot.

botToken.label: Bot token
botToken.hint: Bot tab → Reset Token to reveal the bot token. Stored encrypted.
botToken.savedHint: A saved bot token is in place (Bot tab → Token). Enter a new value to replace it.

publicKey.label: Public key
publicKey.hint: General Information page → Public Key. Verifies interaction signatures. Stored encrypted.
publicKey.savedHint: A saved public key is in place (General Information → Public Key). Enter a new value to replace it.

intents.label: Gateway intents
intents.hint: Enable matching intents under Bot → Privileged Gateway Intents (e.g. MESSAGE CONTENT) or message bodies arrive empty.

errors.appIdInUse: This Discord application ID is already bound to another workspace.
errors.incomplete: The Discord connector is incomplete. Fill in all required fields.
errors.generic: Failed to save the Discord connector. Please try again.

teams.title: Microsoft Teams connector
teams.description: Bind one Teams bot for this workspace. All agents reachable from this workspace share this bot.

docLink.label: Register a bot in the Azure Bot / App registrations portal

enabled.label: Enabled
enabled.hint: Turn on to route Teams messages through this bot.

appId.label: App ID
appId.hint: Azure App registrations → your app → Application (client) ID.

appPassword.label: App password
appPassword.hint: Azure App registrations → Certificates & secrets → Client secret value. Stored encrypted.
appPassword.savedHint: A saved app password is in place (Certificates & secrets → Client secret). Enter a new value to replace it.

tenantId.label: Tenant ID
tenantId.hint: Optional. Directory (tenant) ID for a single-tenant bot; leave empty for a multi-tenant (botframework.com) bot.

errors.appIdInUse: This Teams app ID is already bound to another workspace.
errors.incomplete: The Teams connector is incomplete. Fill in all required fields.
errors.generic: Failed to save the Teams connector. Please try again.

scheduledTasks.title: Scheduled tasks
scheduledTasks.subtitle: At each fire time, runs the agent once in a clean session; results land in run history.
scheduledTasks.empty: No scheduled tasks yet — create one from the top right.
scheduledTasks.noAgents: This project has no available agents yet — create and enable an agent before configuring scheduled tasks.
scheduledTasks.deleteConfirm: Delete this scheduled task? Past runs are kept.

dialog.name: Name
dialog.promptPlaceholder: Summarise yesterday's commits, list today's TODOs…
dialog.feishuDisabledHint: Coming in a later release; Web-only for now.
dialog.cronInvalid: cron must have 5 fields (min hour dom mon dow)
